meta.note: Pre-market Monday pull — US-listed cash equities (TLT, EWY, semis, financials) still reflect Friday Jul 3's close since the cash session hasn't opened. FX, rates, gold, VIX futures, and Asia equities are live: USDJPY broke above the 162.00 BOJ threshold overnight, USDKRW round-tripped back above the 1,530 Korea-gate line, and KOSPI gave back part of Friday's rebound.

bias: Pre-market Monday: US cash equities (TLT, EWY, semis, financials) are still marking Friday July 3's close, so the real signal today is in FX, rates, and Asia. USDJPY broke above the 162.00 BOJ threshold overnight — the first close above that line this episode — while USDKRW round-tripped back above the 1,530 Korea-gate line after briefly dipping below it Sunday. KOSPI gave back part of Friday's sharp rebound (-0.46% to 8,051), pulling the Korea gate back to 0/3 conditions met from Friday's 1/3. JP10Y rose another 6.5bp, narrowing the US/Japan 10Y spread to roughly 162bps and adding to carry-unwind pressure alongside the yen move. Elsewhere the picture is calmer: SOFR eased 2bp to sit 1bp below IORB, Treasury yields ticked down 2-3bp (mild bull-flattening), and VIX's up-tick (+3.9% to 16.44) is modest in absolute terms. No new H.4.1 print — TGA, RRP, and reserves are unchanged since last week's read. Read all of this with caution until the US cash session actually opens and EWY, semis, and financials get a live print.

counterargs.thesis: The weekend FX/Asia moves (USDJPY above 162, USDKRW above 1,530, KOSPI pullback) are a genuine regime shift, not just pre-market noise
supporting: USDJPY closed above 162.00 for the first time this episode — a real BOJ-intervention-risk threshold, not a random tick
USDKRW's round-trip back above 1,530 came on the same Asia session as KOSPI's pullback — a coherent 'Korea re-weakening' story
US/Japan 10Y spread narrowed 8-9bp overnight — consistent with genuine carry-trade pressure
JP10Y's rise is a continuation of several sessions of JGB yields climbing, not a one-off
against: US cash equities, EWY, and the semis chain are still unrevised from Friday — no confirmation yet that risk assets will actually react
Sunday-Monday Asia FX volume is thin; the prior pull already flagged an 'unconfirmed' sub-1,530 print that has now simply round-tripped back
VIX's +3.9% tick is small in absolute terms (16.44, still well under 20) — not itself signaling stress
SOFR eased and Treasury yields ticked down — the domestic funding/rates picture actually looks calmer, not worse
counterargs.read: The most tradable read right now is in FX and JGBs, not US equities, until the cash session opens. Treat the Korea gate's slip back to 0/3 as confirmation that Friday's KOSPI rebound was the outlier rather than a new trend — but revisit once EWY and the enabling-layer chain get a live US print.

trade_table.rationale: Chain still marking Friday's rout (DRAM -7.9%, GLW -10.8%, AMKR -12.9%) pre-market. Wait for a live US print before reassessing.
10Y eased ~2bp overnight, SOFR now 1bp below IORB. Modest, unconfirmed until the cash session opens.
USDKRW back above 1,530 and KOSPI -0.46% pulled the gate back to 0/3 from Friday's 1/3. EWY itself still below its 197.06 SMA20.
ALLY/CACC/COF unrevised pre-market at Friday's stabilized levels. HY OAS steady at 2.75%.
USDJPY broke above 162.00 and DXY firmed +0.24% overnight — the dollar-weakness case needs a rethink if this holds into the session.
HYG and OAS unrevised pre-market at 2.75%. No fresh stress signal.
Jul-Sep reserve-drain window still ahead structurally; no new H.4.1 print to offset it this week.

Quarterly Refunding Announcement — auction sizes and TGA guidance for Q3.
